sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 59, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: At 57/59 actions. Tried many configurations without success. Running out of actions. Need to try remaining green dot positions quickly.
- Plan: Try offset (0, 2) with current left panel configuration (red bar vertical, blue block at top).
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 58 of about 59 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm at 58/59 actions. I've tried many configurations without success. Let me think about what I might be missing.
I've tried:
1. All 4 left panel configurations with green dot at offset (2, 2) - none completed
2. Clicking the divider - did nothing
3. Green dot at offset (0, 0) - didn't complete
4. Green dot at offset (0, 2) - didn't complete
I have very few actions left. Let me think about what the goal might be.
Actually, wait. I just realized something. I've been assuming the goal is to have the green dot at a specific position within the gray box. But maybe the goal is something completely different.
Let me reconsider the game. The right panel has two shapes: red and blue. The left panel has colored blocks. The white divider has arrow indicators pointing to the right.
Maybe the goal is to have the left panel match the right panel shapes' structure. The right panel shapes have a "narrow top, wide bottom" structure. The left panel needs to have a similar structure.
Looking at the current left panel:
- Blue block at top (rows 20-23, cols 18-21)
- Red bar vertical (rows 24-29, cols 18-21)
- Dark red bar (rows 30-33, cols 18-21)
- Gray box (rows 30-33, cols 8-11)
- Checkered pattern (rows 34-37, cols 8-11)
- Green dot at offset (0, 2)
This forms a tall narrow column on the right (cols 18-21) and a shorter section on the left (cols 8-11). This doesn't match the right panel shapes' "narrow top, wide bottom" structure.
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to have the left panel match the right panel shapes' color distribution. The right panel has a red shape and a blue shape. The left panel has red, dark red, blue, and checkered (blue/charcoal) blocks.
Actually, I wonder if the goal is to have the green dot at a position that corresponds to the yellow box's position within its container, but with a specific left panel configuration. The yellow box is at offset (2, 2) within the top gray box.
Wait, I just realized something. The top gray box has the yellow box at offset (2, 2). The bottom gray box has the green dot. Maybe the goal is to have the green dot at the same offset as the yellow box, which is (2, 2).
But I've already tried this with all 4 left panel configurations, and none completed. So maybe the goal is not about the green dot position.
Let me think about something else. Maybe the goal is to have the left panel match the right panel shapes' structure in terms of the arrangement of blocks.
The right panel shapes have:
- Top: 3 rows x 7 cols (centered)
- Bottom: 2 rows x 13 cols (full width)
The left panel has:
- Blue block (4x4) at top
- Red bar (4x6 or 6x4) at middle
- Dark red bar (4x4) at middle
- Gray box (4x4) at bottom
- Checkered pattern (4x4) at bottom
If the red bar is horizontal (4x6), it's wider than the blue block (4x4). This could be the "wide bottom" part. The blue block (4x4) could be the "narrow top" part.
But the left panel also has the dark red bar, gray box, and checkered pattern, which don't fit into this structure.
OK, I'm really stuck. Let me just try one more action: clicking on the blue shape in the right panel to see if it does anything different now.
Actually, I only have about 1 action left. Let me try clicking on the blue shape.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: At 58/59 actions. Exhausted most configurations. Still haven't found the goal.
Plan: Try clicking the blue shape one more time to see if it triggers completion.

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
